title: Free Fire Max Tournament App (MVP)

requirements.text: Create the core app navigation and page structure for a Free Fire Max Tournament App with these primary sections: Home (upcoming tournaments), Tournaments (browse/search), Tournament Details, Create Tournament (admin/organizer), My Registrations, and Profile.
acceptanceCriteria: App has a working navigation layout that lets users reach all primary sections.
Routes/pages render without placeholder errors and use English UI text.
Tournament list and details views are wired to data queries (even if seeded with initial sample data until backend is complete).

requirements.text: Implement Internet Identity sign-in and a signed-in user experience (showing the user as authenticated, and gating organizer/admin actions like creating/editing tournaments behind authentication).
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users can browse tournaments but cannot create/edit tournaments or register.
Authenticated users can view their profile and register for tournaments.
UI clearly indicates signed-in/signed-out state in English.

requirements.text: Implement backend data models and persistence for tournaments, including: tournament name, description, rules, region, start date/time, registration open/close date/time, max slots (teams/squads), entry type (solo/duo/squad/custom), prize details (text), organizer principal, and tournament status (draft/open/locked/started/completed/cancelled).
acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes query/update methods to create, read, list, and update tournaments.
Tournaments persist across canister upgrades using stable storage patterns appropriate for a single-actor Motoko canister.
Tournament status transitions are validated (e.g., cannot register when not open).

requirements.text: Implement tournament registration flows for players/teams: allow an authenticated user to register, capture in-game name (IGN), optional team name, and squad member IGNs (up to the tournament’s team size), and allow the user to view/cancel their registration when registration is open.
acceptanceCriteria: A user can register for an open tournament and sees confirmation in the UI.
Registration is blocked when the tournament is full or registration is closed/locked.
My Registrations page shows the user’s registrations and their status.
Organizer can view a list of registered entries for their tournament.

requirements.text: Add match/bracket management for organizers: ability to create match rounds (at least manual round creation), assign registered teams/players into matches, record match results (placement/points or winner), and display a read-only bracket/round view for participants.
acceptanceCriteria: Organizer can create at least one round and add matches for a tournament.
Organizer can assign registrations to matches and submit results.
Participants can view rounds/matches and results on the Tournament Details page.
Backend enforces that only the organizer (or an admin role if implemented) can modify matches/results.

requirements.text: Provide an organizer/admin dashboard for managing a tournament: edit tournament info, open/close registrations, lock the bracket, and publish announcements (text updates) shown on the Tournament Details page.
acceptanceCriteria: Organizer can change tournament status (open/close/lock/start/complete/cancel) via UI controls.
Announcements can be created and are visible to all viewers of the tournament details.
Non-organizers cannot access organizer controls.

requirements.text: Apply a coherent, distinctive visual theme across the app (colors, typography, spacing, and components) suitable for an esports tournament experience, avoiding blue/purple as primary brand colors.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme is consistent across all primary pages and components.
Primary palette is not blue/purple; contrast and readability are acceptable.
UI uses Tailwind + existing UI components by composition (no edits to read-only component sources).

requirements.text: Generate and include required static images as frontend assets and use them in the UI (header/hero and basic branding). Store them under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference them directly from the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images exist under frontend/public/assets/generated with the specified filenames.
Home page and/or app header uses the generated images without routing through the backend.
Assets load correctly in the built app.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in the main actor file).
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui; compose them instead.
Do not edit immutable hook/bootstrap paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT (e.g., frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x), frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx).
Use English for all user-facing text.
nonGoals: Real-time chat, WebSockets, or live match updates.
Integrations with external game APIs or third-party auth providers beyond Internet Identity.
Automated bracket seeding algorithms beyond basic manual assignment for the MVP.

required: Esports tournament shield logo with subtle flame motif, dark background friendly (ffm-tournament-logo.dim_512x512.png)
Wide hero banner with abstract esports arena lighting, warm accent colors (ffm-hero-banner.dim_1600x600.png)
